name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro (Non-Think)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (1.6T total / 49B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention, Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections, Muon optimizer
note: Fast/low-latency mode of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro preview checkpoint, the default reasoning tier a user gets without selecting anything. (fast/low-latency mode; the default tier a user gets without selecting anything).

results.notes: Terminal-Bench 2.0, Non-Think mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
SWE Pro (Resolved), Non-Think mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).

Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, IFBench (instruction following) row for the plain 'DeepSeek V4 Pro' (non-reasoning) model page — distinct from the higher 76% scored by the separate 'DeepSeek V4 Pro (max)' reasoning-tier page. Verified live via the site's Intelligence Breakdown table (DeepSeek V4 Pro = 46%). Cross-check: the same page's GPQA Diamond (72%) and HLE (8%) rows for this tier are consistent with the corpus's existing self-reported 72.9/7.7 values, so those were NOT re-added.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via DeepSeek's own official API-docs news post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news260424/, dated 2026-04-24) announcing the V4-Pro/V4-Flash preview, and via the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro Hugging Face model card, which gives 1.6T total/49B active params, MIT license, 1M-token context, and a benchmark table covering three reasoning tiers (Non-Think/Think High/Think Max). Numerous SEO/content-farm sites (codersera, sitepoint, morphllm, etc.) published speculative-sounding 'release timeline' articles with suspiciously precise but unverifiable claims (e.g. percentile rankings, an 'Intelligence Index' score) — those were NOT used; all numbers here come from the official HF model card table.

2026-07-16: Researched the five missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) for this Non-Think tier. Checked (1) the official deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro HF model card and the DeepSeek-V4 technical report (arXiv:2606.19348) — the instruct-tier tables report MMLU-Pro (not standard MMLU), GPQA Diamond, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces, SWE-bench/SWE-Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.0, BrowseComp, SimpleQA-Verified, HMMT, IMOAnswerBench, MCPAtlas, Toolathlon and similar 2026-era discriminating benchmarks, with no row for standard MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval (instruct tiers), or MMMU; (2) Vals.ai's DeepSeek V4 Pro page, which likewise tracks no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/MMMU entries; and (3) all three sibling V4 catalog entries (Think High, Think Max, V4-Flash Non-Think), none of which carry these five slugs either, confirming a systemic, family-wide absence rather than a gap specific to this checkpoint. A third-party technical summary (redreamality.com) independently corroborates that AIME 2025 and MMLU-Pro were dropped from Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index v4.0 in January 2026 'precisely because frontier models had pushed them past the point where scores could discriminate' — i.e. deliberate deprecation of saturated legacy benchmarks, not an oversight. Note: a HumanEval score of 76.8% (BenchLM, checked live 2026-07-16) exists, but only for the separate 'DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Base' pretrain checkpoint — a categorically different, non-instruct model that is not tracked anywhere in this corpus — so it was correctly NOT attributed to this instruct-tuned Non-Think entry. MMMU is additionally inapplicable on its face: this model is text-only (capabilities.vision=false, modalities=["text"]), so a multimodal image-understanding benchmark cannot apply. All five gaps are omitted as genuine, well-evidenced absences.
